title_en: ‘The Destruction of the Civil Service Is Our National Loss’
title_zh: “特朗普对文职体系的摧毁，是我们国家的共同损失”
rubric_en: Readers respond to our February 2026 issue and more.
rubric_zh: 读者对我们2026年2月刊以及更多内容的回应

paragraphs.en: Donald Trump’s destruction of the civil service is a tragedy not just for the roughly 300,000 workers who have been discarded, but for an entire nation, Franklin Foer wrote in the February issue.
paragraphs.zh: 弗兰克林·福尔在二月刊中写道，唐纳德·特朗普对文职体系的毁灭不仅是对被淘汰出的约30万名公务员的悲剧，更是对整个国家的悲剧。

paragraphs.en: I read Franklin Foer’s “The Purged” in one held breath. This is how to weigh the stakes of our political moment—one life at a time. No statistic can adequately describe America’s losses. Each of the 50 people profiled in Foer’s essay is so gifted and generous, so essential to what the United States means—or at least to what it used to mean. I salute Foer’s courage to tell the story head-on. His narrative journalism is letting us see one another whole.
paragraphs.zh: 我一口气读完了弗兰克林·福尔的《The Purged》。这是衡量我们当前政治时刻利害关系的一种方式：一口气只看一个人。任何统计数字都无法充分描述美国所失去的一切。福尔文章中每一个被采访的50人都如此有才华、如此慷慨，如此对“美国意味着什么”——或至少“美国过去意味着什么”——不可或缺。我敬佩福尔那种正面讲述故事的勇气。通过他的叙事新闻写作，我们得以完整地彼此看见。

paragraphs.en: Rita Charon New York, N.Y.
paragraphs.zh: 丽塔·夏伦 纽约，纽约州

paragraphs.en: There but for the grace of God go I. I never worked directly for the federal government, but I did research and consulting as a contractor for the Department of Energy’s National Laboratories, the Environmental Protection Agency, and the National Center for Atmospheric Research. I am a meteorologist, an atmospheric physicist, and an environmental economist, now retired. I worked on significant problems, including air quality, acid deposition, and the safety of liquefied gases. I developed simulation techniques for the accidental release of toxic chemicals. I don’t know the people in the article personally, but I do know them—I know their value. Their individual loss is our national loss.
paragraphs.zh: 要不是上帝的恩典，那里会是我。我从未直接为联邦政府工作，但我曾作为承包商，为能源部国家实验室、环境保护署和国家大气研究中心做过研究和咨询。我是一名气象学家、大气物理学家和环境经济学家，现在已经退休。我研究过一些重大问题，包括空气质量、酸沉降以及液化气体的安全。我还开发过有毒化学品意外泄漏的模拟技术。我并不认识文章里那些人，但我又认识他们，因为我知道他们的价值。他们每个人的损失，都是我们国家的共同损失。

paragraphs.en: Daniel J. McNaughton Newport, R.I.
paragraphs.zh: Daniel J. McNaughton Newport, R.I.

paragraphs.en: I worked in state government for 29 years. Franklin Foer’s 50 profiles are an important reminder that we’ll feel the consequences of Donald Trump’s purge for decades. I tend to think of government work as that of quiet competence. When we travel on interstate highways, we do not think about the work done to locate an appropriate site for a road, to build it, and then to maintain it. When we fly, we hope that the plane meets safety standards but spare little thought for the federal staff who developed those standards and enforce them for our benefit.
paragraphs.zh: 我在州政府工作了29年。弗兰克林·福尔的《50》篇人物报道提醒我们，唐纳德·特朗普的清洗行动其后果会让我们在几十年后仍深受影响。我始终认为政府工作就是那种低调却有力的职业能力。我们在州际高速上行驶时，并不会想到为选址、修建再维护道路所做的工作。当我们乘坐飞机时，希望飞机符合安全标准，却很少去想那些制订并执行这些标准、为我们服务的联邦工作人员。

paragraphs.en: I was reminded of the importance of federal expertise last year, when the remnants of Hurricane Helene roared through the mountains and left astounding damage to public and private infrastructure. The Mitchell News-Journal, the local paper in rural Mitchell County, North Carolina, has on multiple occasions reported on confusion among employees of the Federal Emergency Management Agency over how to follow the agency’s own internal guidelines. I am sure that FEMA can and should be made more efficient. But destroying morale and stirring chaos with on-again, off-again layoffs is certainly not the way to do it.
paragraphs.zh: 我去年再一次意识到联邦专业能力的重要性，那时飓风海伦的余波席卷群山，给公共和私人基础设施造成了惊人的破坏。北卡罗来纳州米切尔县的地方报纸《米切尔新闻报》在多次报道中提到，联邦应急管理署员工对如何遵循该机构自身内部指南一度感到困惑。我确信FEMA完全可以也应该变得更高效，但靠反复开除与复用来打击士气、制造混乱，绝不是正确做法。

paragraphs.en: John Dorney Durham, N.C.
paragraphs.zh: John Dorney Durham, N.C.

paragraphs.en: I am a retired federal civil servant and a disabled veteran. I worked for the U.S. Postal Service for many years. I always took great pride in my desire to go above and beyond to help others get what they wanted or needed. On behalf of the millions of current and former federal civil-service employees, I am truly grateful for Franklin Foer’s research and reporting in this article.
paragraphs.zh: 我是一名退休的联邦公务员，也是位残疾退伍军人。我曾在美国邮政局工作了许多年。我一直以自己总是尽力而为、帮助他人获得他们想要或需要的东西而感到自豪。代表数百万现任和前任联邦公务员，我对富兰克林·弗尔（Franklin Foer）在这篇文章中的研究与报道表达由衷的感谢。

paragraphs.en: In my 32-year career, the other civil-service employees and members of the military I worked alongside were dedicated, honest, and hardworking professionals. When I started to hear claims that federal employees were overpaid poor performers, or that they supported a particular political agenda that undermined the administration’s, or that they amounted to an unelected, overly partisan bureaucracy, I was shocked. These claims were so the opposite of my own experience that I thought surely anyone hearing them would disregard them as false.
paragraphs.zh: 在我32年的职业生涯中，与我一起工作的其他公务员和军人都很敬业、诚实，并且勤奋工作。当我开始听到一些说法——联邦雇员薪水过高、工作表现差，或者他们支持某种削弱行政当局的特定政治议程，抑或他们只是一个不经选举、过于偏党的官僚体系——时，我十分震惊。这些说法与我的亲身经历完全相反，我当时真诚地认为，任何认真听完的人都会把它们当作谣言而不屑一顾。

paragraphs.en: Nyleen Mullally Rapid City, S.D.
paragraphs.zh: Nyleen Mullally Rapid City, S.D.

paragraphs.en: I was very moved by the depth of reporting in Franklin Foer’s story on purged government employees. The profiles helped fill in the picture—that these are real people, and that their absence will be felt for a very long time. The damage to our culture and our progress as a nation is much larger than many people realize. Thank you for helping us keep that fact front of mind, now and into the future. I hope some of this strength can be rebuilt—but first we’ll need to rebuild the reputation of government.
paragraphs.zh: 我被富兰克林·弗尔关于被清退政府雇员的报道深深打动。文章中的人物简介使整幅图景更加立体——这些都是真实的人，他们的缺席将会在很长一段时间内被人切实感受到。我们国家的文化与国家进步所遭受的损害，比许多人意识到的要大得多。感谢你帮助我们始终把这一事实放在心里、放在眼前，并将其延续到未来。我希望其中一些力量能被重建——但首先，我们必须重建的是政府的信誉。

paragraphs.en: Evelyn Luengas Fort Worth, Texas
paragraphs.zh: Evelyn Luengas，德克萨斯州沃斯堡

paragraphs.en: “I was holding myself together, but just barely,” Sherry Winfield confesses in “Dinah’s Hat,” a new short story by Stephen King. Sherry and her friend Morris have just ventured from their trailer park to a beach with Dinah, the mysterious child Morris cares for; there, they encounter a group of young bullies. “In time,” Sherry recalls thinking of the events that follow, “I’ll be able to convince myself that never happened.”
paragraphs.zh: 在斯蒂芬·金的新短篇《迪娜的帽子》中，谢里·温菲尔德坦言道：“我当时勉强撑着，几乎快要崩溃。”谢里和她的朋友莫里斯刚刚带着神秘的孩子迪娜从拖车营地来到海滩——这是莫里斯照料的孩子；在那里，他们遭遇了一群年轻的恶霸。“后来，”回想随后的事件时，谢里想，“我会让自己相信，那件事根本没发生过。”

paragraphs.en: “Dinah’s Hat” isn’t the first work of fiction King has written for The Atlantic. In the magazine’s May 2011 issue, he published “Herman Wouk Is Still Alive,” a short story told from the perspective of two aging poets, Phil and Pauline, picnicking at a rest stop in Maine, and two mothers, Brenda and Jasmine, eager to take their children on a road trip up I-95. Compared with much of King’s other work, the horrors of “Herman Wouk Is Still Alive” are fairly quotidian—dead-end jobs and deadbeat dads, aging, depression. The story, nevertheless, builds to a haunting conclusion.
paragraphs.zh: 《迪娜的帽子》并不是金在《大西洋月刊》发表的第一部小说作品。在该刊2011年5月号中，他发表了《赫曼·沃克仍然活着》，这是一篇由年迈诗人菲尔与保琳视角叙述的短篇，故事发生在缅因州一个休息站野餐时；同时间，两个母亲——布伦达和茉莉珊——急着带孩子们沿95号州际公路踏上公路旅行。与金大部分作品中的恐怖相比，《赫曼·沃克仍然活着》里的恐惧相对寻常——例如前途无望的工作、缺席的父亲、衰老与抑郁。尽管如此，故事仍然走向了令人难以摆脱的、萦绕人心的结尾。

paragraphs.en: In a 2011 interview with The Atlantic’s James Parker, King explained that the story had emerged from a bet with his son Owen on that year’s NCAA basketball tournament: The winner would provide a title for a short story; the loser would have to write it. Owen, victorious, proffered the phrase “Herman Wouk Is Still Alive,” inspired by news coverage he’d read that Wouk, the Pulitzer Prize–winning author of War and Remembrance and The Caine Mutiny, was still writing well into his 90s. In King’s eventual story, Phil and Pauline read a fictionalized New York Times article testifying to Wouk’s longevity. “The ideas don’t stop just because one is old,” Wouk tells his interviewer. “The body weakens, but the words never do.” His sentiment inspires the two poets, both in their 70s—until, moments later, tragedy renders language powerless.
paragraphs.zh: 在2011年接受《大西洋月刊》James Parker采访时，金解释说，这篇故事源于他与儿子欧文在当年NCAA篮球赛事期间打的一场赌：胜者给短篇起标题，败者则必须写作。欧文赢了，便给出了“赫曼·沃克仍然活着”这个短语，灵感来自他读到的一篇新闻：普利策奖得主赫曼·沃克——《战争与回忆》《凯恩叛乱》一书的作者——在九十多岁时仍在持续写作。后来在金的故事里，菲尔和保琳读到了一篇虚构化的《纽约时报》文章，用以证明沃克的长寿。沃克对采访者说：“人的思想不会因为年龄大就停止运转。身体会衰弱，但文字却不会。” 这句话鼓舞了这对七十多岁的诗人，而就在片刻之后，一场悲剧让语言也变得无力。

paragraphs.en: Wouk himself was asked about King’s story in a Q&A published with his 2012 novel, The Lawgiver. “I read Mr. King’s short story and enjoyed it,” Wouk said. “As for the longevity, I share his evident puzzlement, with boundless gratitude to my forebears and my Maker.” He continued, “It helps to have work I love, with much work yet to do by His grace.” Wouk died in 2019, just 10 days before his 104th birthday.
paragraphs.zh: 沃克本人在2012年小说《立法者》随附的问答中被问及金先生的故事。他说：“我读过金先生的这篇短篇小说，很喜欢它。”他又说：“至于其持久性，我和他一样困惑，并满怀感激于我的先辈和我的造物主。”他接着说：“有一份自己热爱的工作，而且在他的恩典下，还有许多工作尚待完成，这很有帮助。”沃克于2019年去世，距离其第104个生日仅10天。

paragraphs.en: — Andrew Aoyama, Deputy Managing Editor
paragraphs.zh: — Andrew Aoyama，副执行编辑

paragraphs.en: In this month’s cover story, “The Men Who Don’t Want Women to Vote,” Helen Lewis argues that “masculinism”—a movement seeking to counter the advances of feminism and place men back at the center of public life—has become the most important uniting force on the American right. Many men are drawn to masculinism, Lewis writes, because they feel that they have lost status to women. For the cover, we depicted the fear that undergirds their resentment: A rough silhouette of a man runs toward the viewer, looking over his shoulder at an imagined threat.
paragraphs.zh: 在本月的封面故事《那些不让女性投票的人》一文中，海伦·刘易斯认为，“男性主义”——一种试图遏制女性主义进展并将男性重新置于公共生活核心的运动——已成为美国右翼中最重要的联合力量。许多男性会被这种男性主义吸引，刘易斯写道，是因为他们觉得自己已经在女性面前失去了地位。对于封面，我们描绘了支撑他们怨懑的恐惧：一个粗糙的人形剪影朝向读者奔跑，并回头窥视着一个想象中的威胁。

paragraphs.en: — Liz Hart, Art Director
paragraphs.zh: — Liz Hart，艺术总监

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the June 2026 print edition with the headline “The Commons.” When you buy a book using a link on this page, we receive a commission. Thank you for supporting The Atlantic.
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章出现在2026年6月的纸质版封面栏目《The Commons》中。当你通过本页的链接购买图书时，我们将获得一笔佣金。感谢你支持《The Atlantic》。
